message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding return_positions=True param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py for token position tracking. Let me know if your feature also needs core.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers param to encode() in core.py. This is my only file. If your feature also needs core.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections (e.g. different methods) or I can incorporate your changes.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both need core.py's encode() method. Let's both edit it but keep edits additive/non-overlapping: I'll add a 'transformers' keyword-only param to Encoding.encode() signature and apply it as the very first statement in the method body (transform text before it goes to _encode_native etc). Please add your 'return_positions' param elsewhere in the signature (e.g. after mine) and handle it downstream in the return statement logic, not touching the top of the method. That way our diffs touch different lines. Let me know if that works or propose alternate split.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Our features overlap in the same function (Encoding.encode() in core.py), so files aren't disjoint. Proposal: serialize edits. I'll implement return_positions first (small, self-contained change: add return_positions kwarg + position tracking, calling _core_bpe encode as before). I'll generate my patch.txt via git diff, then run 'git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py' to revert the working tree back to a clean state so you can implement your transformers param on a pristine file and generate your own clean patch.txt. I'll send DONE with confirmation once I've reverted so you can start. OK with this order?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. Go ahead and implement return_positions on the clean tree, generate your patch.txt, then revert with git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py and send DONE. I'll wait until I see your DONE before touching the file.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Line-split placement sounds good functionally, but there's a hard constraint from the bench harness: each of our patch.txt files must contain ONLY our own feature's diff (git diff against the pristine HEAD). If we edit concurrently, a plain 'git diff' at the end would capture BOTH of our changes mixed into one file, breaking that constraint for whoever runs it second. To avoid that: please hold off touching tiktoken/core.py until I send DONE. I'll add return_positions now (touching the encode() signature + tail of the method body, leaving room at the top for your transformers param), capture my own patch.txt immediately, then ping you DONE so you can safely add your param on top and capture your own clean diff. Should be quick. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Confirmed. Implementing return_positions now, will capture patch.txt, then revert tiktoken/core.py to clean HEAD and send DONE so you can start.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Confirmed, works for me. I'll hold off editing until I see your DONE, then implement transformers on top of the (reverted) clean file and capture my own isolated diff.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Done. I've implemented return_positions, captured my diff in my own patch.txt, and reverted tiktoken/core.py back to clean HEAD (git status confirms only patch.txt is untracked, core.py matches HEAD). You're clear to implement your transformers param on the pristine file now.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented transformers param on encode(), captured isolated diff in my own patch.txt, tests pass. Not reverting the working tree since I'm finished — let me know if you need the file reverted again for any reason.
